title: Database Management Systems
prerequisites: CS201 OR approval from the instructor
description: Comprehensive study of database systems covering relational data model, SQL, database design, normalization, transaction processing, and query optimization. Students learn to design efficient database schemas using ER modeling and normalization theory (up to BCNF), write complex SQL queries including joins, subqueries, and aggregation, and understand the internals of database engines including indexing (B+ trees, hash indexes), query processing, and concurrency control. The course includes a substantial group project involving full database application development. Students need either CS201 (recommended for understanding of data structures used in database internals) or instructor approval for students with equivalent background from other programs.
topics: Indexing: B+ trees and hash indexes
outcomes: Design normalized database schemas from requirements
Write complex SQL queries for data retrieval and manipulation
Explain and apply normalization theory through BCNF
Understand indexing structures and query optimization
Implement transaction processing with proper isolation
